title: Support of non-hevc base layer in hevc multi-layer extensions
abstract: An apparatus configured to decode video information includes a memory and a processor in communication with the memory. The memory is configured to a memory configured to store video information associated with bitstream. The apparatus further includes a processor in communication with the memory, the processor configured to determine that a reference layer is not included in the bitstream and to receive, from an external source, a decoded base layer picture. The processor is further configured to receive, from the external source, a first indication that the picture is an intra random access point (IRAP) picture. The processor is also configured to receive a second indication whether the picture is one of an instantaneous decoder refresh (IDR) picture, a clean random access (CRA) picture, or a broken link access (BLA) picture; and to decode the video information based at least in part on the first and second indications.
claims:
Claims What is claimed is; 1. A method of decoding video information, the method comprising: determining that a reference layer is not included in a bitstream; receiving, from an external source, a decoded base layer picture; receiving, from the external source, a first indication that the picture is an intra random access point (IRAP) picture; receiving a second indication whether the picture is one of an instantaneous decoder refresh (IDR) picture, a clean random access (CRA) picture, or a broken link access (BLA) picture; and decoding the video information based at least in part on the first and second indications. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein the reference layer is decoded according to a non-HEVC (High Efficiency Video Coding) codec. 3. The method of claim 1, wherein the reference layer is decoded according to an Advanced Video Coding (AVC) codec. 4. The method of claim 1, wherein determining whether the reference layer is included in the bitstream comprises determining whether the reference layer is included in the bitstream based upon a value included in a one of a video parameter set, a sequence parameter set, a picture parameter set, or an adaptation parameter set. 5. The method of claim 1, wherein the external source is a second decoder. 6. The method of claim 5, wherein the second decoder is configured to decode an AVC base layer. 7. The method of claim 1, wherein the indication comprises a flag having a value of either 1 or 0 to indicate that the picture is the IRAP picture. 8. The method of claim 1, wherein the second indication comprises a syntax element with three or more possible values. 9. An apparatus configured to decode video information, the apparatus comprising: a memory configured to store video information associated with bitstream; a processor in communication with the memory, the processor configured to: determine that a reference layer is not included in the bitstream; receive, from an external source, a decoded base layer picture; receive, from the external source, a first indication that the picture is an intra random access point (FRAP) picture; receive a second indication whether the picture is one of an instantaneous decoder refresh (IDR) picture, a clean random access (CRA) picture, or a broken link access (BLA) picture; and decode the video information based at least in part on the first and second indications. 10. The apparatus of claim 9, wherein the reference layer is decoded according to a non-HEVC (High Efficiency Video Coding) codec. 11. The apparatus of claim 9, wherein the reference layer is decoded according to an Advanced Video Coding (AVC) codec. 12. The apparatus of claim 9, wherein determining whether the reference layer is included in the bitstream comprises determining whether the reference layer is included in the bitstream based upon a value included in a one of a video parameter set, a sequence parameter set, a picture parameter set, or an adaptation parameter set. 13. The apparatus of claim 9, wherein the external source is a second decoder. 14. The apparatus of claim 13, wherein the second decoder is configured to decode an AVC base layer. 15. The apparatus of claim 9, wherein the indication comprises a flag which may be set to either 1 or 0 to indicate that the picture is the IRAP picture. 16. The apparatus of claim 9, wherein the second indication comprises a syntax element with three or more possible values. 17. A non-transitory computer readable medium comprising code that, when executed, causes an apparatus to perform a process comprising: determining that a reference layer is not included in a bitstream; receiving, from an external source, a decoded base layer picture; receiving, from the external source, a first indication that the picture is an intra random access point (IRAP) picture; receiving a second indication whether the picture is one of an instantaneous decoder refresh (IDR) picture, a clean random access (CRA) picture, or a broken link access (BLA) picture; and decoding the video information based at least in part on the first and second indications. 18. The non-transitory computer readable medium of claim 17, wherein the reference layer is decoded according to at least one of a non-HEVC (High Efficiency Video Coding) codec and an Advanced Video Coding (AVC) codec. 19. The non-transitory computer readable medium of claim 17, wherein the external source is a second decoder. 20. The non-transitory computer readable medium of claim 17, wherein the indication comprises a flag which may be set to either 1 or 0 to indicate that the picture is the IRAP picture. 21. The non-transitory computer readable medium of claim 17, wherein the second indication comprises a syntax element with three or more possible values. 22. A video decoding device configured to decode video information, the video coding device comprising: means for determining that a reference layer is not included in a bitstream; means for receiving, from an external source, a decoded base layer picture; means for receiving, from the external source, a first indication that the picture is an intra random access point (IRAP) picture; 

 means for receiving a second indication, if the picture is an IRAP picture, whether the picture is one of an instantaneous decoder refresh (IDR) picture, a clean random access (CRA) picture, or a broken link access (BLA) picture; and means for decoding the video information based at least in part on the first and second indications. 23. The video decoding device of claim 22, wherein the reference layer is decoded according to at least one of a non-HEVC (High Efficiency Video Coding) codec and an Advanced Video Coding (AVC) codec. 24. The video decoding device of claim 22, wherein the external source is a second decoder. 25. The video decoding device of claim 24, wherein the second decoder is configured to decode an AVC base layer. 26. The video decoding device of claim 22, wherein the indication comprises a flag having a value of either 1 or 0 to indicate that the picture is the IRAP picture. 27. The video decoding device of claim 22, wherein the second indication comprises a syntax element with three or more possible values.
